nodes.name: GitHub API — Axe-core Rules
nodes.notes: Source 1: Lists axe-core rule files via GitHub contents API (1 call), then fetches each rule's JSON from raw.githubusercontent.com (no rate limit) in parallel batches. Emits normalized items with id, description, help, helpUrl, tags. helpUrl is synthesized from the rule id since axe-core rule JSON does not include it directly.

nodes.name: W3C WCAG JSON — Success Criteria
nodes.notes: Source 2: W3C official WCAG 2.1 guidelines as structured JSON from the W3C GitHub repository. Static file — extremely reliable. Returns all 78 success criteria with SC number, title, level, and description.

nodes.name: RSS — WebAIM Blog
nodes.notes: Source 3a: WebAIM Blog RSS feed. WebAIM is the leading web accessibility research organization. Feed returns article titles, dates, URLs, and summaries. No auth required.

nodes.name: RSS — A11y Project
nodes.notes: Source 3b: The A11y Project RSS feed. Practitioner-focused accessibility community. Parallel to WebAIM — if one feed is down, the other continues independently.

nodes.name: Merge + Normalize + Deduplicate
nodes.notes: Excellence node: Merges all 3 sources into canonical schema. Normalizes dates to YYYY-MM-DD. Deduplicates by record_id. Validates completeness — flags incomplete records rather than dropping them.

nodes.name: Quality Gate — Min 50 Records
nodes.notes: Excellence node: Guards against bad runs overwriting good data. If fewer than 50 records collected (meaning multiple sources failed), routes to error log instead of saving. Protects the existing clean CSV.

nodes.name: Save to CSV
nodes.notes: Saves merged, cleaned data as CSV. Filename includes date stamp (accessibility_data_YYYY-MM-DD.csv) so previous runs are preserved. Also writes accessibility_data_latest.csv via a secondary Write File node.

assignments.name: error_message
assignments.value: ={{ 'Pipeline error at ' + $now.toISO() + ' — only ' + $items().length + ' records collected. Check source availability and re-run.' }}

nodes.name: Write Error Log
nodes.notes: Runs when fewer than 50 records were collected. Writes error details to pipeline_error_log.txt. Does NOT overwrite the existing clean CSV — protects good data.
